{
  "claims": [
    "California State University (CSU) has returned only six percent of Native American remains and objects at its campuses since the passage of the 1990 Native American Graves Protection and Repatriation Act (NAGPRA).",
    "The number of collections of Native American remains and cultural items at CSU campuses is almost 700,000 and is expected to grow.",
    "Twelve of the 21 CSU campuses with collections have not finished the reviews required by NAGPRA.",
    "Sixteen CSU campuses have little or no repatriation activity.",
    "Two CSU campuses returned remains without following NAGPRA notification requirements.",
    "The Chancellor’s Office has not provided necessary guidance, oversight, and funding to CSU campuses for repatriation efforts.",
    "CSU campuses lack oversight, knowledge, funding, and staff to advance the repatriation of Native American remains and cultural items."
  ]
}
